mapping.a_element: interference dynamics in quantum scattering (collision of Gaussian wave packets)
mapping.b_element: matter wave interference pattern from coherently split Bose-Einstein condensates

mapping.a_element: caustics and vortices as singularities governing trajectory topologies in the complex plane
mapping.b_element: spatial phase of the interference pattern as a signature of relative phase evolution

mapping.a_element: complex quantum Hamilton-Jacobi formalism describing interference
mapping.b_element: coherent phase evolution observed in the interferometer

brief_justification: While both abstracts involve quantum interference, paper A focuses on singularities (caustics and vortices) in the complex quantum Hamilton-Jacobi formalism as the governing mechanism, whereas paper B relies on coherent phase evolution in a double-well potential and spatial interference patterns. The underlying mathematical machinery (complex trajectory analysis vs. trapped-atom interferometry) differs fundamentally.
